items.title: How to Implement OAuth in Rust - Shuttle.dev
items.snippet:
How to Implement OAuth in Rust

Joshua Mo• 30 August 2023

### Stay updated  ...    In this post, we'll be learning how to implement OAuth 2.0 in Rust by writing a backend service that will interact with Google OAuth and will interact with OpenID Connect("OIDC") service from Google to retrieve a user's email. We'll first learn to use the oauth2 library to authorise our users using database-backed sessions to keep them authenticated with a private cookie jar, then we'll use a middleware for Rust authentication to  ...    The final code for the repository can be found here.

## Set Up#  ...    - A project in Google Cloud Console (you can get started here- it's free!)  ...    ## Getting Started#

To get started, you'll want to create a new project by running the following: ```bash
shuttle init

``` We'll want to pick "axum" as the framework. For the purposes of the project we will refer to the project name as "oauth-rust". Looking to deploy? Make sure you enable initialising your project on the Shuttle servers!  ...    ```bash
cargo add axum --features multipart,macros
cargo add axum-extra --features cookie-private  ...    ```rust
// main.rs
use reqwest::Client as ReqwestClient;
use sqlx::PgPool;
use axum::extract::{cookie::Key, FromRef};  ...    #[shuttle_runtime::main]
async fn axum(
    #[shuttle_shared_db::Postgres] db: PgPool,  ...    let oauth_id = secrets.get("GOOGLE_OAUTH_CLIENT_ID").unwrap();  ...    TokenError(
        #[from]
        oauth2::RequestTokenError<
            oauth2::reqwest::Error<reqwest::Error>,  ...    response.into_response()
    }
}

```

## But how do I use OAuth?# First, we will need to write a function to create an`oauth2::BasicClient`. This client can take any OAuth authorization endpoint URL and token endpoint URL (as long as they're both from the same OAuth service). We can pass in our Google OAuth secrets that we created earlier, as you'll be able to see below. Our redirect URL should be an endpoint that we  ...    ```rust
// src/main.rs
use oauth::{TokenUrl, AuthUrl,
    basic::BasicClient,
    ClientId, ClientSecret,  ...    fn build_oauth_client(client_id: String, client_secret: String) -> BasicClient { // In prod, http://localhost:8000 would get replaced by whatever your production URL is  ...    let auth_url = AuthUrl::new("https://accounts.google.com/o/oauth2/v2/auth".to_string())  ...    ```rust
// src/routes/oauth.rs
use axum_extra::extract::cookie::PrivateCookieJar;
use axum::extract::{State, Query};  ...    was given to us (that we required permissions for, as per the redirect URI). Thankfully, this is pretty simple to do without the oauth2 crate and we only need to use a simple`Reqwest` client with bearer auth to get the user profile  ...    Matthias Endler@matthiasendlerRust Consultant @ Corrode

## Using OAuth with Axum Extensions#  ...    ```rust
#[shuttle_runtime::main]
async fn axum(
    #[shuttle_shared_db::Postgres] db: PgPool, #[shuttle_secrets::Secrets] secrets: SecretStore,
) -> shuttle_axum::ShuttleAxum { sqlx::migrate!().run(&db).await.expect("Failed migrations :("); let oauth_id = secrets.get("GOOGLE_OAUTH_CLIENT_ID").unwrap();  ...    let ctx = Client::new();

    let state = AppState {
        db,
        ctx,
        key: Key::generate()
    }; let oauth_client = build_oauth_client(oauth_id.clone(), oauth_secret);  ...    fn init_router(state: AppState, oauth_client: BasicClient, oauth_id: String) -> Router {  ...    let protected_router = Router::new()
       .route("/", get(oauth::protected)).route_layer(middleware::from_fn_with_state(state.clone(), oauth::check_authenticated));  ...    ```

## Deploying to Production#  ...    Thanks for reading! I hope you enjoyed this guide to impl  ementing OAuth in Rust and leveraging the oauth2 library for Rust auth.  ...    Best Rust Web Frameworks to Use in 202323 August 2023 •

# Rust Axum OAuth 2.0 Signup and Login with Axum Middleware
LTM LABS PVT LTD

LTM LABS PVT LTD
Follow
3 min read
·Aug 16, 2024

 --

Share
# Introduction In the world of modern web development, security is paramount, and OAuth 2.0 is one of the most popular standards for secure user authentication. Rust, known for its safety and performance, is quickly becoming a go-to language for backend developers. In this article, we’ll explore how to implement OAuth 2.0 signup and login using the Axum framework and its middleware capabilities. Whether you’re building a new web application or integrating OAuth 2.0 into an existing Rust project, this guide will walk you through the process step-by-step, complete with code examples. # Why Axum? Axum is a web framework built on top of theTowerlibrary, offering a powerful and flexible way to build web applications in Rust. It provides first-class support for async, making it a great choice for performance-critical  ...    # Key Features of Axum: * **Type-Safe Routing**: Axum ensures that your routes are fully type-safe, reducing runtime errors. * **Middleware Support**: Axum’s middleware system allows you to implement reusable components for tasks like logging, authentication, and more. * **Integration with Tower**: Axum seamlessly integrates with Tower…

VintageWander / ** simple-axum-google-oauth ** Public * Notifications You must be signed in to change notification settings * Fork 0 * Star 2 * A simple example on how to use Google OAuth2 with Axum Rust, but only the backend is needed, no need for the frontend 2 stars 0 forks Branches Tags Activity [ Star ... # VintageWander/simple-axum-google-oauth ... .cargo | .cargo | | | | prisma-cli | prisma-cli | | | | ... readme.md | | | | View all files | ## Repository files navigation # Simple Axum Google OAuth2 Example This is a very simple example of how you can integrate Google OAuth2 into your `axum` backend This example only contains the backend, as in this case no frontend is needed # The code structure: ``` simple-axum-google-oauth ├── prisma │ └── schema.prisma # Database schema ├── prisma-cli │ ├── src │ │ └── main.rs # Prima's CLI │ └── Cargo.toml ├── src │ ├── routes # Endpoints ... ├── Dockerfile # Rust container ├── docker-compose.dev.yml # Starts Postgres ... └── readme.md ``` # Authentication flow ``` sequenceDiagram Browser ->> Backend: Calls localhost:5000/login?return_url=/profile Backend -->> Browser: Sends a redirect response Browser ->> Google: Redirects to Google's server Google -->> Browser: Prompts user about the app's access to user's data Browser ->> Google: Accepts the prompt ... Backend ->> Backend: Note over Backend,Backend: Use user's email
to create a local account
with randomized password ... ``` # How to deploy Customize your values, in the `docker-compose` file. And then: ``` docker-compose -f docker-compose.prod.yml up -d ``` ## About A simple example on how to use Google OAuth2 with Axum Rust, but only the backend is needed, no need for the frontend ### Resources Readme Activity ### Stars **2** stars ### Watchers [ **1** ...
